turns.text:
Implementing all planned changes across the cloud_sky plugin files.
Running build verification and updating the todo and walkthrough files.
turns.thinking:
The plan adds `sun_temperature` as a separate field in `SkyParamsUniform`. The current struct packs fields as Vec4 for WebGL2 alignment.

A standalone f32 would break the Vec4 packing and WebGL2 alignment. The plan requires updating both WGSL shaders to match. Alternatively, temperature could be packed into an unused component of an existing vec4 such as `amounts.w`.

The struct totals 64 bytes. Adding a standalone f32 would require padding. Bevy's ShaderType should handle alignment automatically. The plan explicitly requires a separate field.

The plan appears to contain a typo: `params.sun_temperature` should be `u.sun_temperature`. The WGSL struct must gain a standalone `f32` field, and `precip_overlay.wgsl` needs matching updates. A standalone `f32` after a `vec4` will require padding for alignment.

The standalone f32 will sit at offset 16, followed by padding through offset 31, then the amounts vec4 at offset 32. Bevy's ShaderType should keep Rust and WGSL layouts consistent. I will add sun_temperature to both structs exactly as the plan specifies.

`make_sky_params` should write `sun_temperature` rather than `sync_sky_uniforms`, since the latter only refreshes when settings change. An `auto_sun_temperature` system must run every frame to update temperature from `time_of_day` and mark settings as changed. It must be registered before `sync_sky_uniforms` and `sync_sun_light`.

ResMut mutations should correctly mark settings as changed. Overwriting sun_temperature every frame during auto mode may be inefficient if time_of_day is static, since identical values could still trigger is_changed. Bevy may not suppress redundant writes.

Auto temperature will only overwrite the uniform when the computed value differs from the current one, avoiding redundant work. The system order is follow_camera, auto_sun_temperature, sync_sky_uniforms, then sync_sun_light. On startup, make_sky_params runs before auto_sun_temperature, so the initial temperature is set immediately.

Default sun_temperature is 5250, matching the auto value at 14.5. The manual temperature slider will be disabled when auto mode is active. Auto mode mutates settings every frame, which triggers sync each frame. The implementation spans settings.rs, ui.rs, and materials.rs.

The implementation touches `systems.rs`, `skybox_clouds.wgsl`, `precip_overlay.wgsl`, and `mod.rs`. `kelvin_to_rgb` and `auto_sun_temperature` go into `systems.rs`, alongside updated `make_sky_params` and `sync_sun_light`. `skybox_clouds.wgsl` needs `kelvin_to_rgb` plus a parameter for `sun_temperature` passed into `sky_color`, while `precip_overlay.wgsl` must declare the new field. `mod.rs` registers the new system. `sky_color` will receive `u.sun_temperature` as a parameter because the fragment shader already accesses the global uniform block.

The default sun_temperature of 5250K does not match the auto curve at time 14.5. The lerp calculation gives 5833K, so the default value must be corrected.

The plan explicitly specifies a default sun_temperature of 5250K. This conflicts with the auto curve at time 14.5, which computes roughly 5833K. Using the stated default risks a color jump on the first Update frame before auto mode corrects it. Computing the true default or running auto on startup would prevent this.

The plan explicitly requires a default sun_temperature of 5250.0, overriding the auto-curve value at time 14.5 to avoid a startup color jump. The sync_sky_uniforms update must be added to the changes section.
